     1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→MTL (Multi-Tenant Analytics Language) - File-based CLI for data transformation pipeline.
     4→Extended version with schemas, inheritance, calculated fields, parameters, and lag/lead.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→import argparse
     8→import csv
     9→import json
    10→import math
    11→import sys
    12→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
    13→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    14→from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
    15→from enum import Enum
    16→from typing import (
    17→    Any, Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Set, Tuple, Union, get_type_hints
    18→)
    19→
    20→
    21→# =============================================================================
    22→# Error Classes
    23→# =============================================================================
    24→
    25→class MTLException(Exception):
    26→    """Base exception for MTL errors."""
    27→    error_type: str
    28→    exit_code: int = 1
    29→
    30→    def __init__(self, message: str):
    31→        self.message = message
    32→        super().__init__(message)
    33→
    34→    def stderr_message(self) -> str:
    35→        return f"ERROR:{self.error_type}:{self.message}"
    36→
    37→
    38→class BadDSLException(MTLException):
    39→    error_type = "bad_dsl"
    40→    exit_code = 1
    41→
    42→
    43→class TypeErrorException(MTLException):
    44→    error_type = "type_error"
    45→    exit_code = 2
    46→
    47→
    48→class BadIOException(MTLException):
    49→    error_type = "bad_io"
    50→    exit_code = 1
    51→
    52→
    53→class RuntimeException(MTLException):
    54→    error_type = "runtime_error"
    55→    exit_code = 4
    56→
    57→
    58→# =============================================================================
    59→# Type System
    60→# =============================================================================
    61→
    62→class ValueType(ABC):
    63→    """Base class for value types in MTL."""
    64→    pass
    65→
    66→
    67→class StringType(ValueType):
    68→    pass
    69→
    70→
    71→class NumberType(ValueType):
    72→    pass
    73→
    74→
    75→class IntType(ValueType):
    76→    pass
    77→
    78→
    79→class TimestampType(ValueType):
    80→    pass
    81→
    82→
    83→class BoolType(ValueType):
    84→    pass
    85→
    86→
    87→@dataclass
    88→class MTLValue:
    89→    """Represents a typed value in MTL."""
    90→    value: Any
    91→    type: ValueType
    92→
    93→    def is_numeric(self) -> bool:
    94→        return isinstance(self.type, NumberType)
    95→
    96→    def is_int(self) -> bool:
    97→        return isinstance(self.type, IntType)
    98→
    99→    def is_string(self) -> bool:
   100→        return isinstance(self.type, StringType)
   101→
   102→    def is_timestamp(self) -> bool:
   103→        return isinstance(self.type, TimestampType)
   104→
   105→    def is_bool(self) -> bool:
   106→        return isinstance(self.type, BoolType)
   107→
   108→    def as_number(self) -> float:
   109→        if self.is_timestamp():
   110→            raise TypeError("Cannot convert timestamp to number")
   111→        return float(self.value)
   112→
   113→    def as_int(self) -> int:
   114→        if self.is_int():
   115→            return self.value
   116→        if self.is_number():
   117→            return int(self.value)
   118→        raise TypeError(f"Cannot convert {type(self.type).__name__} to int")
   119→
   120→    def as_string(self) -> str:
   121→        return str(self.value)
   122→
   123→    def as_timestamp(self) -> datetime:
   124→        if self.is_timestamp():
   125→            return self.value
   126→        raise TypeError("Not a timestamp")
   127→
   128→    def as_bool(self) -> bool:
   129→        return bool(self.value)
   130→
   131→
   132→def types_compatible(from_type: ValueType, to_type: ValueType) -> bool:
   133→    """Check if from_type can be assigned to to_type."""
   134→    if isinstance(from_type, type(to_type)):
   135→        return True
   136→    # Numeric widening: int -> NumberType (which includes float)
   137→    if isinstance(from_type, IntType) and isinstance(to_type, NumberType):
   138→        return True
   139→    return False
   140→
   141→
   142→def can_widen(from_type: ValueType, to_type: ValueType) -> bool:
   143→    """Check if a type override is a valid widening."""
   144→    if isinstance(from_type, type(to_type)):
   145→        return True  # Same type is always allowed
   146→    # int -> float widening is allowed
   147→    if isinstance(from_type, IntType) and isinstance(to_type, NumberType):
   148→        return True
   149→    # float -> NumberType is same (float IS NumberType)
   150→    if isinstance(from_type, NumberType) and isinstance(to_type, NumberType):
   151→        return True
   152→    return False
   153→
   154→
   155→# =============================================================================
   156→# Expression System
   157→# =============================================================================
   158→
   159→@dataclass
   160→class Expression(ABC):
   161→    """Base class for all MTL expressions."""
   162→    @abstractmethod
   163→    def eval(self, context: Dict[str, MTLValue]) -> MTLValue:
   164→        pass
   165→
   166→    @abstractmethod
   167→    def get_type(self, schema: Dict[str, ValueType]) -> ValueType:
   168→        pass
   169→
   170→    @abstractmethod
   171→    def is_deterministic(self, params: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   172→        """Check if expression is deterministic given params."""
   173→        return True
   174→
   175→
   176→@dataclass
   177→class LiteralExpr(Expression):
   178→    value: MTLValue
   179→
   180→    def eval(self, context: Dict[str, MTLValue]) -> MTLValue:
   181→        return self.value
   182→
   183→    def get_type(self, schema: Dict[str, ValueType]) -> ValueType:
   184→        return self.value.type
   185→
   186→    def is_deterministic(self, params: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   187→        return True
   188→
   189→
   190→@dataclass
   191→class ColumnRefExpr(Expression):
   192→    name: str
   193→
   194→    def eval(self, context: Dict[str, MTLValue]) -> MTLValue:
   195→        if self.name not in context:
   196→            raise RuntimeException(f"Unknown column: {self.name}")
   197→        return context[self.name]
   198→
   199→    def get_type(self, schema: Dict[str, ValueType]) -> ValueType:
   200→        if self.name not in schema:
   201→            raise TypeErrorException(f"Unknown column: {self.name}")
   202→        return schema[self.name]
   203→
   204→    def is_deterministic(self, params: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   205→        return True
   206→
   207→
   208→@dataclass
   209→class ParamRefExpr(Expression):
   210→    name: str
   211→
   212→    def eval(self, context: Dict[str, MTLValue]) -> MTLValue:
   213→        # Params are passed via closure/capture, not context
   214→        raise RuntimeException(f"Param '{self.name}' must be resolved before evaluation")
   215→
   216→    def get_type(self, schema: Dict[str, ValueType]) -> ValueType:
   217→        # Type checked during pipeline setup
   218→        return NumberType()  # placeholder
   219→
   220→    def eval_with_params(self, params: Dict[str, MTLValue]) -> MTLValue:
   221→        if self.name not in params:
   222→            raise RuntimeException(f"Parameter '{self.name}' not provided")
   223→        return params[self.name]
   224→
   225→    def is_deterministic(self, params: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   226→        return self.name in params
   227→
   228→
   229→@dataclass
   230→class BinaryOpExpr(Expression):
   231→    left: Expression
   232→    op: str
   233→    right: Expression
   234→
   235→    def eval(self, context: Dict[str, MTLValue]) -> MTLValue:
   236→        lv = self.left.eval(context)
   237→        rv = self.right.eval(context)
   238→
   239→        if self.op in ("<", "<=", ">", ">=", "==", "!="):
   240→            return self._compare(lv, rv)
   241→        elif self.op == "&":
   242→            return MTLValue(lv.as_bool() and rv.as_bool(), BoolType())
   243→        elif self.op == "|":
   244→            return MTLValue(lv.as_bool() or rv.as_bool(), BoolType())
   245→        elif self.op in ("+", "-", "*", "/", "**"):
   246→            if not (lv.is_numeric() and rv.is_numeric()):
   247→                raise TypeErrorException(f"Numeric operation '{self.op}' requires numeric operands")
   248→            result = self._numeric_op(lv.as_number(), rv.as_number(), self.op)
   249→            if math.isnan(result) or math.isinf(result):
   250→                raise RuntimeException(f"Numeric operation resulted in NaN/Inf: {self.op}")
   251→            return MTLValue(result, NumberType())
   252→        elif self.op == "in":
   253→            if isinstance(lv.value, str) and isinstance(rv.value, str):
   254→                return MTLValue(lv.value in rv.value, BoolType())
   255→            else:
   256→                raise TypeErrorException("'in' requires string literal on left and string column on right")
   257→        else:
   258→            raise RuntimeException(f"Unknown operator: {self.op}")
   259→
   260→    def _compare(self, lv: MTLValue, rv: MTLValue) -> MTLValue:
   261→        # Allow comparison between compatible numeric types
   262→        if lv.is_numeric() and rv.is_numeric():
   263→            result = self._numeric_compare(lv.as_number(), rv.as_number(), self.op)
   264→            return MTLValue(result, BoolType())
   265→        if lv.is_string() and rv.is_string():
   266→            result = self._string_compare(lv.value, rv.value, self.op)
   267→            return MTLValue(result, BoolType())
   268→        if lv.is_timestamp() and rv.is_timestamp():
   269→            result = self._timestamp_compare(lv.value, rv.value, self.op)
   270→            return MTLValue(result, BoolType())
   271→        if lv.is_bool() and rv.is_bool():
   272→            result = self._general_compare(lv.as_bool(), rv.as_bool(), self.op)
   273→            return MTLValue(result, BoolType())
   274→        raise TypeErrorException(f"Cannot compare {type(lv.type).__name__} with {type(rv.type).__name__}")
   275→
   276→    def _string_compare(self, a: str, b: str, op: str) -> bool:
   277→        if op == "<": return a < b
   278→        if op == "<=": return a <= b
   279→        if op == ">": return a > b
   280→        if op == ">=": return a >= b
   281→        if op == "==": return a == b
   282→        if op == "!=": return a != b
   283→        return False
   284→
   285→    def _numeric_compare(self, a: float, b: float, op: str) -> bool:
   286→        if op == "<": return a < b
   287→        if op == "<=": return a <= b
   288→        if op == ">": return a > b
   289→        if op == ">=": return a >= b
   290→        if op == "==": return a == b
   291→        if op == "!=": return a != b
   292→        return False
   293→
   294→    def _timestamp_compare(self, a: datetime, b: datetime, op: str) -> bool:
   295→        if op == "<": return a < b
   296→        if op == "<=": return a <= b
   297→        if op == ">": return a > b
   298→        if op == ">=": return a >= b
   299→        if op == "==": return a == b
   300→        if op == "!=": return a != b
   301→        return False
   302→
   303→    def _general_compare(self, a: Any, b: Any, op: str) -> bool:
   304→        if op == "==": return a == b
   305→        if op == "!=": return a != b
   306→        raise TypeErrorException(f"Cannot use '{op}' for boolean comparison")
   307→
   308→    def _numeric_op(self, a: float, b: float, op: str) -> float:
   309→        if op == "+": return a + b
   310→        if op == "-": return a - b
   311→        if op == "*": return a * b
   312→        if op == "/":
   313→            if b == 0:
   314→                raise RuntimeException("Division by zero")
   315→            return a / b
   316→        if op == "**": return a ** b
   317→        raise RuntimeException(f"Unknown numeric operator: {op}")
   318→
   319→    def get_type(self, schema: Dict[str, ValueType]) -> ValueType:
   320→        lt = self.left.get_type(schema)
   321→        rt = self.right.get_type(schema)
   322→
   323→        if self.op in ("<", "<=", ">", ">=", "==", "!=", "in"):
   324→            return BoolType()
   325→        elif self.op in ("&", "|"):
   326→            if not (isinstance(lt, BoolType) and isinstance(rt, BoolType)):
   327→                raise TypeErrorException(f"Logical operation requires boolean operands")
   328→            return BoolType()
   329→        elif self.op in ("+", "-", "*", "/", "**"):
   330→            if not (lt.is_numeric() and rt.is_numeric()):
   331→                raise TypeErrorException(f"Arithmetic operation requires numeric operands")
   332→            return NumberType()
   333→        else:
   334→            raise TypeErrorException(f"Unknown operator: {self.op}")
   335→
   336→    def is_deterministic(self, params: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   337→        return self.left.is_deterministic(params) and self.right.is_deterministic(params)
   338→
   339→
   340→@dataclass
   341→class UnaryOpExpr(Expression):
   342→    op: str
   343→    operand: Expression
   344→
   345→    def eval(self, context: Dict[str, MTLValue]) -> MTLValue:
   346→        val = self.operand.eval(context)
   347→        if self.op == "!":
   348→            if not val.is_bool():
   349→                raise TypeErrorException(f"'!' requires boolean operand, got {type(val.type).__name__}")
   350→            return MTLValue(not val.as_bool(), BoolType())
   351→        else:
   352→            raise RuntimeException(f"Unknown unary operator: {self.op}")
   353→
   354→    def get_type(self, schema: Dict[str, ValueType]) -> ValueType:
   355→        if self.op == "!":
   356→            t = self.operand.get_type(schema)
   357→            if not isinstance(t, BoolType):
   358→                raise TypeErrorException(f"'!' requires boolean operand")
   359→            return BoolType()
   360→        raise TypeErrorException(f"Unknown unary operator: {self.op}")
   361→
   362→    def is_deterministic(self, params: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   363→        return self.operand.is_deterministic(params)
   364→
   365→
   366→@dataclass
   367→class TransformExpr(Expression):
   368→    """Represents a transform function call like len(col), day(col), etc."""
   369→    func: str
   370→    arg: Expression
   371→    digits: Optional[Expression] = None
   372→
   373→    def eval(self, context: Dict[str, MTLValue]) -> MTLValue:
   374→        arg_val = self.arg.eval(context)
   375→
   376→        if self.func == "len":
   377→            if not arg_val.is_string():
   378→                raise TypeErrorException("len() requires string argument")
   379→            return MTLValue(len(arg_val.value), NumberType())
   380→
   381→        elif self.func in ("day", "month", "year"):
   382→            if not arg_val.is_timestamp():
   383→                raise TypeErrorException(f"{self.func}() requires timestamp argument")
   384→            ts = arg_val.as_timestamp()
   385→            if self.func == "day":
   386→                return MTLValue(ts.day, IntType())
   387→            elif self.func == "month":
   388→                return MTLValue(ts.month, IntType())
   389→            else:
   390→                return MTLValue(ts.year, IntType())
   391→
   392→        elif self.func == "round":
   393→            if not arg_val.is_numeric():
   394→                raise TypeErrorException("round() requires numeric argument")
   395→            digits_val = 0
   396→            if self.digits is not None:
   397→                d = self.digits.eval(context)
   398→                if not d.is_numeric():
   399→                    raise TypeErrorException("round() digits must be integer")
   400→                digits_val = int(d.as_number())
   401→                if digits_val != d.as_number():
   402→                    raise TypeErrorException("round() digits must be integer")
   403→            val = arg_val.as_number()
   404→            return MTLValue(round_half_away_from_zero(val, digits_val), NumberType())
   405→
   406→        elif self.func == "starts_with":
   407→            if not (isinstance(arg_val.value, str)):
   408→                raise TypeErrorException("starts_with() requires string argument")
   409→            # Second arg is passed differently - needs special handling
   410→            raise RuntimeException("starts_with() requires two arguments")
   411→
   412→        elif self.func == "ends_with":
   413→            if not (isinstance(arg_val.value, str)):
   414→                raise TypeErrorException("ends_with() requires string argument")
   415→            raise RuntimeException("ends_with() requires two arguments")
   416→
   417→        elif self.func == "contains":
   418→            if not (isinstance(arg_val.value, str)):
   419→                raise TypeErrorException("contains() requires string argument")
   420→            raise RuntimeException("contains() requires two arguments")
   421→
   422→        else:
   423→            raise RuntimeException(f"Unknown transform function: {self.func}")
   424→
   425→    def get_type(self, schema: Dict[str, ValueType]) -> ValueType:
   426→        arg_type = self.arg.get_type(schema)
   427→
   428→        if self.func == "len":
   429→            if not isinstance(arg_type, StringType):
   430→                raise TypeErrorException("len() requires string argument")
   431→            return IntType()
   432→
   433→        elif self.func in ("day", "month", "year"):
   434→            if not isinstance(arg_type, TimestampType):
   435→                raise TypeErrorException(f"{self.func}() requires timestamp argument")
   436→            return IntType()
   437→
   438→        elif self.func == "round":
   439→            if not isinstance(arg_type, NumberType):
   440→                raise TypeErrorException("round() requires numeric argument")
   441→            if self.digits is not None:
   442→                digits_type = self.digits.get_type(schema)
   443→                if not digits_type.is_numeric():
   444→                    raise TypeErrorException("round() digits must be integer")
   445→            return NumberType()
   446→
   447→        else:
   448→            raise TypeErrorException(f"Unknown transform function: {self.func}")
   449→
   450→    def is_deterministic(self, params: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   451→        return self.arg.is_deterministic(params) and (self.digits.is_deterministic(params) if self.digits else True)
   452→
   453→
   454→@dataclass
   455→class LagLeadExpr(Expression):
   456→    """Represents lag/lead function for post-aggregation."""
   457→    func: str  # 'lag' or 'lead'
   458→    expr: Expression
   459→    k: int
   460→    is_post_agg: bool = False
   461→
   462→    def eval(self, context: Dict[str, MTLValue]) -> MTLValue:
   463→        if not self.is_post_agg:
   464→            raise RuntimeException(f"{self.func}() can only be used in post_agg stage")
   465→        if "_laglead_index" not in context:
   466→            raise RuntimeException(f"{self.func}() requires sorted results")
   467→        idx = context["_laglead_index"].as_int()
   468→        results = context["_laglead_results"].value
   469→
   470→        target_idx = idx + (self.k if self.func == "lead" else -self.k)
   471→        if target_idx < 0 or target_idx >= len(results):
   472→            return MTLValue(None, NumberType())  # Null for out of bounds
   473→
   474→        result_val = results[target_idx]
   475→        expr_name = self.expr.name if hasattr(self.expr, 'name') else None
   476→        if expr_name and expr_name in result_val:
   477→            val = result_val[expr_name]
   478→            if isinstance(val, (int, float)):
   479→                return MTLValue(val, NumberType())
   480→            return MTLValue(val, StringType() if isinstance(val, str) else NumberType())
   481→        return MTLValue(None, NumberType())
   482→
   483→    def get_type(self, schema: Dict[str, ValueType]) -> ValueType:
   484→        return self.expr.get_type(schema)
   485→
   486→    def is_deterministic(self, params: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   487→        return True
   488→
   489→
   490→@dataclass
   491→class CoalesceExpr(Expression):
   492→    """Represents coalesce(a, b) function."""
   493→    left: Expression
   494→    right: Expression
   495→
   496→    def eval(self, context: Dict[str, MTLValue]) -> MTLValue:
   497→        lv = self.left.eval(context)
   498→        rv = self.right.eval(context)
   499→
   500→        # Check if left is null/None
   501→        if lv.value is not None:
   502→            return lv
   503→        return rv
   504→
   505→    def get_type(self, schema: Dict[str, ValueType]) -> ValueType:
   506→        lt = self.left.get_type(schema)
   507→        rt = self.right.get_type(schema)
   508→        if isinstance(lt, type(rt)):
   509→            return lt
   510→        raise TypeErrorException(f"coalesce() types must match: {type(lt).__name__} vs {type(rt).__name__}")
   511→
   512→    def is_deterministic(self, params: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   513→        return self.left.is_deterministic(params) and self.right.is_deterministic(params)
   514→
   515→
   516→@dataclass
   517→class CastExpr(Expression):
   518→    """Represents cast(expr as type)."""
   519→    expr: Expression
   520→    target_type: ValueType
   521→
   522→    def eval(self, context: Dict[str, MTLValue]) -> MTLValue:
   523→        val = self.expr.eval(context)
   524→        return self._cast_value(val, self.target_type)
   525→
   526→    def _cast_value(self, val: MTLValue, target: ValueType) -> MTLValue:
   527→        if isinstance(target, type(val.type)):
   528→            return val
   529→        if isinstance(target, NumberType) and val.is_numeric():
   530→            return MTLValue(val.as_number(), NumberType())
   531→        if isinstance(target, IntType) and val.is_numeric():
   532→            return MTLValue(int(val.as_number()), IntType())
   533→        if isinstance(target, StringType):
   534→            return MTLValue(val.as_string(), StringType())
   535→        if isinstance(target, BoolType):
   536→            return MTLValue(val.as_bool(), BoolType())
   537→        if isinstance(target, TimestampType) and val.is_string():
   538→            # Try to parse as timestamp
   539→            raise RuntimeException(f"Cannot cast string to timestamp: {val.value}")
   540→        raise TypeErrorException(f"Cannot cast {type(val.type).__name__} to {type(target).__name__}")
   541→
   542→    def get_type(self, schema: Dict[str, ValueType]) -> ValueType:
   543→        # Just verify expr can be cast to target type
   544→        src_type = self.expr.get_type(schema)
   545→        if not self._can_cast(src_type, self.target_type):
   546→            raise TypeErrorException(f"Cannot cast {type(src_type).__name__} to {type(self.target_type).__name__}")
   547→        return self.target_type
   548→
   549→    def _can_cast(self, src: ValueType, target: ValueType) -> bool:
   550→        if isinstance(target, type(src)):
   551→            return True
   552→        if isinstance(target, NumberType) and src.is_numeric():
   553→            return True
   554→        if isinstance(target, IntType) and src.is_numeric():
   555→            return True
   556→        if isinstance(target, StringType):
   557→            return True
   558→        if isinstance(target, BoolType):
   559→            return True
   560→        return False
   561→
   562→    def is_deterministic(self, params: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   563→        return self.expr.is_deterministic(params)
   564→
   565→
   566→@dataclass
   567→class BinaryStringFuncExpr(Expression):
   568→    """Represents binary string functions: starts_with, ends_with, contains."""
   569→    func: str
   570→    left: Expression
   571→    right: Expression
   572→
   573→    def eval(self, context: Dict[str, MTLValue]) -> MTLValue:
   574→        lv = self.left.eval(context)
   575→        rv = self.right.eval(context)
   576→
   577→        if not isinstance(lv.value, str):
   578→            raise TypeErrorException(f"{self.func}() requires string as first argument")
   579→        if not isinstance(rv.value, str):
   580→            raise TypeErrorException(f"{self.func}() requires string as second argument")
   581→
   582→        if self.func == "starts_with":
   583→            return MTLValue(lv.value.startswith(rv.value), BoolType())
   584→        elif self.func == "ends_with":
   585→            return MTLValue(lv.value.endswith(rv.value), BoolType())
   586→        elif self.func == "contains":
   587→            return MTLValue(rv.value in lv.value, BoolType())
   588→        else:
   589→            raise RuntimeException(f"Unknown string function: {self.func}")
   590→
   591→    def get_type(self, schema: Dict[str, ValueType]) -> ValueType:
   592→        lt = self.left.get_type(schema)
   593→        rt = self.right.get_type(schema)
   594→        if not isinstance(lt, StringType):
   595→            raise TypeErrorException(f"{self.func}() requires string as first argument")
   596→        if not isinstance(rt, StringType):
   597→            raise TypeErrorException(f"{self.func}() requires string as second argument")
   598→        return BoolType()
   599→
   600→    def is_deterministic(self, params: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   601→        return self.left.is_deterministic(params) and self.right.is_deterministic(params)
   602→
   603→
   604→def round_half_away_from_zero(value: float, decimals: int) -> float:
   605→    """Round half away from zero (like Python 3's round)."""
   606→    if decimals == 0:
   607→        return round(value)
   608→    multiplier = 10 ** decimals
   609→    return round(value * multiplier) / multiplier
   610→

   616→@dataclass
   617→class SchemaField:
   618→    """Represents a field in a schema."""
   619→    name: str
   620→    type: ValueType
   621→    expr: Optional[Expression] = None  # Calculated field expression
   622→    is_calculated: bool = False
   623→
   624→
   625→@dataclass
   626→class Schema:
   627→    """Represents a named schema with fields and inheritance."""
   628→    name: str
   629→    base_name: Optional[str]  # None if no base
   630→    fields: Dict[str, SchemaField] = field(default_factory=dict)
   631→    calculated_fields: Dict[str, SchemaField] = field(default_factory=dict)
   632→
   633→    def get_field(self, name: str) -> Optional[SchemaField]:
   634→        return self.fields.get(name)
   635→
   636→    def get_type(self, name: str) -> Optional[ValueType]:
   637→        field = self.fields.get(name)
   638→        return field.type if field else None
   639→
   640→
   641→class SchemaRegistry:
   642→    """Manages all schemas and resolves inheritance."""
   643→
   644→    def __init__(self):
   645→        self.schemas: Dict[str, Schema] = {}
   646→
   647→    def add_schema(self, schema: Schema):
   648→        """Add a schema to the registry."""
   649→        if schema.name in self.schemas:
   650→            raise BadDSLException(f"Duplicate schema name: {schema.name}")
   651→        self.schemas[schema.name] = schema
   652→
   653→    def resolve_inheritance(self, schema_name: str) -> Schema:
   654→        """Resolve a schema with full inheritance."""
   655→        visited = set()
   656→        return self._resolve_inheritance_impl(schema_name, visited)
   657→
   658→    def _resolve_inheritance_impl(self, schema_name: str, visited: Set[str]) -> Schema:
   659→        """Recursively resolve inheritance with cycle detection."""
   660→        if schema_name in visited:
   661→            raise BadDSLException(f"Circular inheritance detected involving: {schema_name}")
   662→        visited.add(schema_name)
   663→
   664→        if schema_name not in self.schemas:
   665→            raise BadDSLException(f"Unknown schema: {schema_name}")
   666→
   667→        schema = self.schemas[schema_name]
   668→
   669→        # Create resolved schema by copying and inheriting
   670→        resolved = Schema(schema.name, schema.base_name)
   671→
   672→        # Inherit from base
   673→        if schema.base_name:
   674→            base_resolved = self._resolve_inheritance_impl(schema.base_name, visited.copy())
   675→            # Copy base fields
   676→            for name, field in base_resolved.fields.items():
   677→                resolved.fields[name] = field
   678→
   679→        # Add/override with child fields
   680→        for name, field in schema.fields.items():
   681→            if name in resolved.fields:
   682→                # Check type override compatibility
   683→                base_field = resolved.fields[name]
   684→                if not can_widen(base_field.type, field.type):
   685→                    raise TypeErrorException(
   686→                        f"Illegal type override for field '{name}': "
   687→                        f"{type(base_field.type).__name__} -> {type(field.type).__name__}"
   688→                    )
   689→            resolved.fields[name] = field
   690→
   691→        return resolved
   692→
