   731→# =============================================================================
   732→# DSL Parser (Extended)
   733→# =============================================================================
   734→
   735→class TokenType(Enum):
   736→    IDENTIFIER = 1
   737→    STRING_LITERAL = 2
   738→    NUMBER = 3
   739→    BOOL = 4
   740→    LPAREN = 5
   741→    RPAREN = 6
   742→    LBRACE = 7
   743→    RBRACE = 8
   744→    LBRACKET = 9
   745→    RBRACKET = 10
   746→    COMMA = 7
   747→    EQ = 8  # "as" or assignment
   748→    COLON = 11
   749→    AT = 12
   750→    COMMENT = 9
   751→    WHITESPACE = 10
   752→    UNKNOWN = 11
   753→    STAR = 12
   754→    SCHEMA = 13
   755→    PIPELINE = 14
   756→    EXTENDS = 15
   757→    USING = 16
   758→    CALC = 17
   759→    PARAMS = 18
   760→    STAGE = 19
   761→
   762→
   763→@dataclass
   764→class Token:
   765→    type: TokenType
   766→    value: str
   767→    line: int
   768→    col: int
   769→
   770→
   771→class Lexer:
   772→    def __init__(self, text: str):
   773→        self.text = text
   774→        self.pos = 0
   775→        self.line = 1
   776→        self.col = 1
   777→        self.tokens: List[Token] = []
   778→
   779→    def tokenize(self) -> List[Token]:
   780→        keywords = {
   781→            "true": TokenType.BOOL, "false": TokenType.BOOL,
   782→            "as": TokenType.EQ, "schema": TokenType.SCHEMA,
   783→            "pipeline": TokenType.PIPELINE, "extends": TokenType.EXTENDS,
   784→            "using": TokenType.USING, "calc": TokenType.CALC,
   785→            "params": TokenType.PARAMS, "stage": TokenType.STAGE,
   786→            "string": TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "int": TokenType.IDENTIFIER,
   787→            "float": TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "bool": TokenType.IDENTIFIER,
   788→            "timestamp": TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "pre": TokenType.IDENTIFIER,
   789→            "post_agg": TokenType.IDENTIFIER,
   790→        }
   791→        operators = {
   792→            "(": TokenType.LPAREN,
   793→            ")": TokenType.RPAREN,
   794→            "{": TokenType.LBRACE,
   795→            "}": TokenType.RBRACE,
   796→            "[": TokenType.LBRACKET,
   797→            "]": TokenType.RBRACKET,
   798→            ",": TokenType.COMMA,
   799→        }
   800→
   801→        while self.pos < len(self.text):
   802→            char = self.text[self.pos]
   803→
   804→            # Skip whitespace
   805→            if char.isspace():
   806→                if char == "\n":
   807→                    self.line += 1
   808→                    self.col = 1
   809→                else:
   810→                    self.col += 1
   811→                self.pos += 1
   812→                continue
   813→
   814→            # Comments
   815→            if char == "#":
   816→                while self.pos < len(self.text) and self.text[self.pos] != "\n":
   817→                    self.pos += 1
   818→                continue
   819→
   820→            # Identifiers and keywords
   821→            if char.isalpha() or char == "_":
   822→                start = self.pos
   823→                while self.pos < len(self.text) and (self.text[self.pos].isalnum() or self.text[self.pos] == "_"):
   824→                    self.pos += 1
   825→                value = self.text[start:self.pos]
   826→                token_type = keywords.get(value.lower(), TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
   827→                self.tokens.append(Token(token_type, value, self.line, self.col - len(value)))
   828→                self.col += len(value)
   829→                continue
   830→
   831→            # Numbers
   832→            if char.isdigit() or (char == "." and self.pos + 1 < len(self.text) and self.text[self.pos + 1].isdigit()):
   833→                start = self.pos
   834→                has_dot = char == "."
   835→                if not has_dot:
   836→                    self.pos += 1
   837→                while self.pos < len(self.text):
   838→                    if self.text[self.pos].isdigit():
   839→                        self.pos += 1
   840→                    elif self.text[self.pos] == "." and not has_dot:
   841→                        has_dot = True
   842→                        self.pos += 1
   843→                    else:
   844→                        break
   845→                value = self.text[start:self.pos]
   846→                self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.NUMBER, value, self.line, self.col))
   847→                self.col += len(value)
   848→                continue
   849→
   850→            # String literals (single quotes)
   851→            if char == "'":
   852→                start = self.pos
   853→                self.pos += 1
   854→                while self.pos < len(self.text) and self.text[self.pos] != "'":
   855→                    if self.text[self.pos] == "\\":
   856→                        self.pos += 2
   857→                    else:
   858→                        self.pos += 1
   859→                if self.pos >= len(self.text):
   860→                    raise BadDSLException(f"Unterminated string literal at line {self.line}")
   861→                self.pos += 1
   862→                value = self.text[start + 1:self.pos - 1]
   863→                self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.STRING_LITERAL, value, self.line, self.col))
   864→                self.col += len(value) + 2
   865→                continue
   866→
   867→            # Multi-character operators
   868→            if char in ("<", ">", "="):
   869→                if self.pos + 1 < len(self.text) and self.text[self.pos + 1] == "=":
   870→                    value = char + "="
   871→                    self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, value, self.line, self.col))
   872→                    self.pos += 2
   873→                    self.col += 2
   874→                    continue
   875→                elif char == "!" and self.pos + 1 < len(self.text) and self.text[self.pos + 1] == "=":
   876→                    value = "!="
   877→                    self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, value, self.line, self.col))
   878→                    self.pos += 2
   879→                    self.col += 2
   880→                    continue
   881→
   882→            # Colon and At
   883→            if char == ":":
   884→                self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.COLON, ":", self.line, self.col))
   885→                self.pos += 1
   886→                self.col += 1
   887→                continue
   888→            if char == "@":
   889→                self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.AT, "@", self.line, self.col))
   890→                self.pos += 1
   891→                self.col += 1
   892→                continue
   893→
   894→            # Single char tokens
   895→            if char in operators:
   896→                self.tokens.append(Token(operators[char], char, self.line, self.col))
   897→                self.pos += 1
   898→                self.col += 1
   899→                continue
   900→
   901→            # Star
   902→            if char == "*":
   903→                self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.STAR, "*", self.line, self.col))
   904→                self.pos += 1
   905→                self.col += 1
   906→                continue
   907→
   908→            # Unknown character
   909→            self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.UNKNOWN, char, self.line, self.col))
   910→            self.pos += 1
   911→            self.col += 1
   912→
   913→        return self.tokens
   914→
   915→
   916→class Parser:
   917→    def __init__(self, tokens: List[Token]):
   918→        self.tokens = tokens
   919→        self.pos = 0
   920→        self.schemas: Dict[str, Schema] = {}
   921→        self.pipeline: Optional[Pipeline] = None
   922→
   923→    def peek(self) -> Optional[Token]:
   924→        if self.pos < len(self.tokens):
   925→            return self.tokens[self.pos]
   926→        return None
   927→
   928→    def consume(self, expected_type: Optional[TokenType] = None, expected_value: Optional[str] = None) -> Token:
   929→        token = self.peek()
   930→        if token is None:
   931→            raise BadDSLException(f"Unexpected end of input")
   932→        if expected_type is not None and token.type != expected_type:
   933→            raise BadDSLException(f"Expected {expected_type}, got {token.type} at line {token.line}")
   934→        if expected_value is not None and token.value != expected_value:
   935→            raise BadDSLException(f"Expected '{expected_value}', got '{token.value}' at line {token.line}")
   936→        self.pos += 1
   937→        return token
   938→
   939→    def parse(self) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Schema], Pipeline]:
   940→        """Parse the entire DSL, returning schemas and pipeline."""
   941→        # Parse schema blocks first
   942→        while self.peek() and self.peek().type == TokenType.SCHEMA:
   943→            self.parse_schema_block()
   944→
   945→        # Parse pipeline block
   946→        if not self.peek() or self.peek().type != TokenType.PIPELINE:
   947→            raise BadDSLException("Expected 'pipeline' block")
   948→        self.parse_pipeline_block()
   949→
   950→        # Ensure end of input
   951→        if self.peek():
   952→            raise BadDSLException(f"Unexpected token after pipeline: {self.peek().value}")
   953→
   954→        return self.schemas, self.pipeline
   955→
   956→    def parse_schema_block(self):
   957→        """Parse a schema block."""
   958→        self.consume(TokenType.SCHEMA, "schema")
   959→        name_token = self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
   960→        name = name_token.value
   961→
   962→        base_name = None
   963→        if self.peek() and self.peek().type == TokenType.EXTENDS:
   964→            self.consume(TokenType.EXTENDS)
   965→            base_token = self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
   966→            base_name = base_token.value
   967→
   968→        self.consume(TokenType.LBRACE)
   969→
   970→        schema = Schema(name, base_name)
   971→
   972→        while self.peek() and self.peek().type != TokenType.RBRACE:
   973→            field = self.parse_schema_field()
   974→            if field.name in schema.fields:
   975→                raise BadDSLException(f"Duplicate field '{field.name}' in schema '{name}'")
   976→            schema.fields[field.name] = field
   977→
   978→        self.consume(TokenType.RBRACE)
   979→
   980→        if name in self.schemas:
   981→            raise BadDSLException(f"Duplicate schema name: {name}")
   982→        self.schemas[name] = schema
   983→
   984→    def parse_schema_field(self) -> SchemaField:
   985→        """Parse a field declaration in a schema."""
   986→        name_token = self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
   987→        name = name_token.value
   988→
   989→        self.consume(TokenType.COLON)
   990→
   991→        type_token = self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
   992→        type_str = type_token.value.lower()
   993→
   994→        if type_str == "string":
   995→            field_type = StringType()
   996→        elif type_str == "int":
   997→            field_type = IntType()
   998→        elif type_str == "float":
   999→            field_type = NumberType()
  1000→        elif type_str == "bool":
  1001→            field_type = BoolType()
  1002→        elif type_str == "timestamp":
  1003→            field_type = TimestampType()
  1004→        else:
  1005→            raise BadDSLException(f"Unknown type: {type_str}")
  1006→
  1007→        expr = None
  1008→        is_calculated = False
  1009→
  1010→        if self.peek() and self.peek().type == TokenType.EQ:
  1011→            self.consume(TokenType.EQ)
  1012→            expr = self.parse_expression()
  1013→            is_calculated = True
  1014→
  1015→        return SchemaField(name, field_type, expr, is_calculated)
  1016→
  1017→    def parse_pipeline_block(self):
  1018→        """Parse a pipeline block."""
  1019→        self.consume(TokenType.PIPELINE, "pipeline")
  1020→        name_token = self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
  1021→        name = name_token.value
  1022→
  1023→        self.consume(TokenType.USING, "using")
  1024→        schema_token = self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
  1025→        schema_name = schema_token.value
  1026→
  1027→        self.consume(TokenType.LBRACE)
  1028→
  1029→        pipeline = Pipeline(name, schema_name)
  1030→
  1031→        # Parse params block if present
  1032→        if self.peek() and self.peek().type == TokenType.PARAMS:
  1033→            self.parse_params_block(pipeline)
  1034→
  1035→        # Parse statements
  1036→        while self.peek() and self.peek().type != TokenType.RBRACE:
  1037→            stmt = self.parse_pipeline_statement()
  1038→            self._add_pipeline_statement(pipeline, stmt)
  1039→
  1040→        self.consume(TokenType.RBRACE)
  1041→
  1042→        if schema_name not in self.schemas:
  1043→            raise BadDSLException(f"Unknown schema referenced by pipeline: {schema_name}")
  1044→
  1045→        self.pipeline = pipeline
  1046→
  1047→    def parse_params_block(self, pipeline: Pipeline):
  1048→        """Parse the params block."""
  1049→        self.consume(TokenType.PARAMS, "params")
  1050→        self.consume(TokenType.LBRACE)
  1051→
  1052→        while self.peek() and self.peek().type != TokenType.RBRACE:
  1053→            name_token = self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
  1054→            name = name_token.value
  1055→
  1056→            param_type = StringType()  # Default type
  1057→            default = None
  1058→
  1059→            if self.peek() and self.peek().type == TokenType.COLON:
  1060→                self.consume(TokenType.COLON)
  1061→                type_token = self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
  1062→                type_str = type_token.value.lower()
  1063→                if type_str == "string":
  1064→                    param_type = StringType()
  1065→                elif type_str == "int":
  1066→                    param_type = IntType()
  1067→                elif type_str == "float":
  1068→                    param_type = NumberType()
  1069→                elif type_str == "bool":
  1070→                    param_type = BoolType()
  1071→                elif type_str == "timestamp":
  1072→                    param_type = TimestampType()
  1073→                else:
  1074→                    raise BadDSLException(f"Unknown param type: {type_str}")
  1075→
  1076→            if self.peek() and self.peek().type == TokenType.EQ:
  1077→                self.consume(TokenType.EQ)
  1078→                default = self.parse_literal()
  1079→                # Type check default
  1080→                if not types_compatible(default.type, param_type):
  1081→                    raise TypeErrorException(
  1082→                        f"Default value type {type(default.type).__name__} incompatible with param type {type(param_type).__name__}"
  1083→                    )
  1084→
  1085→            pipeline.params[name] = PipelineParam(name, param_type, default)
  1086→
  1087→        self.consume(TokenType.RBRACE)
  1088→
  1089→    def parse_literal(self) -> MTLValue:
  1090→        """Parse a literal value."""
  1091→        token = self.peek()
  1092→        if token.type == TokenType.STRING_LITERAL:
  1093→            self.consume()
  1094→            return MTLValue(token.value, StringType())
  1095→        if token.type == TokenType.NUMBER:
  1096→            self.consume()
  1097→            val = float(token.value)
  1098→            if val == int(val):
  1099→                return MTLValue(int(val), IntType())
  1100→            return MTLValue(val, NumberType())
  1101→        if token.type == TokenType.BOOL:
  1102→            self.consume()
  1103→            return MTLValue(token.value.lower() == "true", BoolType())
  1104→        raise BadDSLException(f"Expected literal, got {token.value}")
  1105→
  1106→    def parse_pipeline_statement(self) -> Dict:
  1107→        """Parse a statement within a pipeline block."""
  1108→        token = self.peek()
  1109→        if token.type == TokenType.CALC:
  1110→            return self.parse_calc()
  1111→        elif token.type == TokenType.IDENTIFIER:
  1112→            if token.value == "filter":
  1113→                return self.parse_filter()
  1114→            elif token.value == "apply":
  1115→                return self.parse_apply()
  1116→            elif token.value == "group_by":
  1117→                return self.parse_group_by()
  1118→            elif token.value == "window":
  1119→                return self.parse_window()
  1120→            elif token.value == "aggregate":
  1121→                return self.parse_aggregate()
  1122→
  1123→        raise BadDSLException(f"Unknown pipeline statement: {token.value}")
  1124→
  1125→    def parse_calc(self) -> Dict:
  1126→        """Parse a calc statement."""
  1127→        self.consume(TokenType.CALC, "calc")
  1128→        name_token = self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
  1129→        name = name_token.value
  1130→
  1131→        self.consume(TokenType.EQ)
  1132→        expr = self.parse_expression()
  1133→
  1134→        self.consume(TokenType.COLON)
  1135→        type_token = self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
  1136→        type_str = type_token.value.lower()
  1137→
  1138→        if type_str == "string":
  1139→            calc_type = StringType()
  1140→        elif type_str == "int":
  1141→            calc_type = IntType()
  1142→        elif type_str == "float":
  1143→            calc_type = NumberType()
  1144→        elif type_str == "bool":
  1145→            calc_type = BoolType()
  1146→        elif type_str == "timestamp":
  1147→            calc_type = TimestampType()
  1148→        else:
  1149→            raise BadDSLException(f"Unknown type: {type_str}")
  1150→
  1151→        self.consume(TokenType.AT)
  1152→        self.consume(TokenType.STAGE, "stage")
  1153→        self.consume(TokenType.LPAREN)
  1154→        stage_token = self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
  1155→        stage = stage_token.value.lower()
  1156→        if stage not in ("pre", "post_agg"):
  1157→            raise BadDSLException(f"Invalid stage: {stage}")
  1158→        self.consume(TokenType.RPAREN)
  1159→
  1160→        return {
  1161→            "type": "calc",
  1162→            "name": name,
  1163→            "expr": expr,
  1164→            "type": calc_type,
  1165→            "stage": stage
  1166→        }
  1167→
  1168→    def _add_pipeline_statement(self, pipeline: Pipeline, stmt: Dict):
  1169→        """Add a parsed statement to the pipeline."""
  1170→        stmt_type = stmt["type"]
  1171→
  1172→        if stmt_type == "filter":
  1173→            if pipeline.filter_expr:
  1174→                raise BadDSLException("Multiple filter statements not allowed")
  1175→            pipeline.filter_expr = stmt["expr"]
  1176→        elif stmt_type == "apply":
  1177→            pipeline.apply_stmts.append((stmt["expr"], stmt["new_col"]))
  1178→        elif stmt_type == "group_by":
  1179→            if pipeline.group_by_cols:
  1180→                raise BadDSLException("Multiple group_by statements not allowed")
  1181→            pipeline.group_by_cols = stmt["cols"]
  1182→        elif stmt_type == "window":
  1183→            if pipeline.window_duration:
  1184→                raise BadDSLException("Multiple window statements not allowed")
  1185→            pipeline.window_duration = stmt["duration"]
  1186→        elif stmt_type == "aggregate":
  1187→            if pipeline.aggregate_spec:
  1188→                raise BadDSLException("Multiple aggregate statements not allowed")
  1189→            pipeline.aggregate_spec = stmt
  1190→        elif stmt_type == "calc":
  1191→            pipeline.calc_stmts.append(CalcStatement(
  1192→                name=stmt["name"],
  1193→                expr=stmt["expr"],
  1194→                type=stmt["type"],
  1195→                stage=stmt["stage"]
  1196→            ))
  1197→
  1198→    def parse_filter(self) -> Dict:
  1199→        self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "filter")
  1200→        self.consume(TokenType.LPAREN)
  1201→        expr = self.parse_boolean_expr()
  1202→        self.consume(TokenType.RPAREN)
  1203→        return {"type": "filter", "expr": expr}
  1204→
  1205→    def parse_apply(self) -> Dict:
  1206→        self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "apply")
  1207→        self.consume(TokenType.LPAREN)
  1208→        expr = self.parse_expression()
  1209→        self.consume(TokenType.COMMA)
  1210→        new_col = self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
  1211→        self.consume(TokenType.RPAREN)
  1212→        return {"type": "apply", "expr": expr, "new_col": new_col.value}
  1213→
  1214→    def parse_group_by(self) -> Dict:
  1215→        self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "group_by")
  1216→        self.consume(TokenType.LPAREN)
  1217→        cols = []
  1218→
  1219→        if self.peek().type == TokenType.LBRACKET:
  1220→            self.consume(TokenType.LBRACKET)
  1221→            cols.append(self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER).value)
  1222→            while self.peek().type == TokenType.COMMA:
  1223→                self.consume(TokenType.COMMA)
  1224→                cols.append(self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER).value)
  1225→            self.consume(TokenType.RBRACKET)
  1226→        else:
  1227→            cols.append(self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER).value)
  1228→
  1229→        self.consume(TokenType.RPAREN)
  1230→        return {"type": "group_by", "cols": cols}
  1231→
  1232→    def parse_window(self) -> Dict:
  1233→        self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "window")
  1234→        self.consume(TokenType.LPAREN)
  1235→        duration = self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
  1236→        self.consume(TokenType.RPAREN)
  1237→        return {"type": "window", "duration": duration.value}
  1238→
  1239→    def parse_aggregate(self) -> Dict:
  1240→        self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "aggregate")
  1241→        aggs = []
  1242→
  1243→        while True:
  1244→            func = self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
  1245→            self.consume(TokenType.LPAREN)
  1246→
  1247→            peek = self.peek()
  1248→            if peek is None or peek.type != TokenType.STAR:
  1249→                col = self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
  1250→                arg = ColumnRefExpr(col.value)
  1251→            else:
  1252→                self.consume(TokenType.STAR)
  1253→                arg = LiteralExpr(MTLValue("*", StringType()))
  1254→
  1255→            self.consume(TokenType.RPAREN)
  1256→            self.consume(TokenType.EQ, "as")
  1257→            alias = self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
  1258→
  1259→            aggs.append({"func": func.value, "arg": arg, "alias": alias.value})
  1260→
  1261→            peek = self.peek()
  1262→            if peek is None or peek.type != TokenType.COMMA:
  1263→                break
  1264→            self.consume(TokenType.COMMA)
  1265→
  1266→        return {"type": "aggregate", "aggs": aggs}
  1267→
  1268→    def parse_boolean_expr(self) -> Expression:
  1269→        return self.parse_or_expr()
  1270→
  1271→    def parse_or_expr(self) -> Expression:
  1272→        left = self.parse_and_expr()
  1273→        while self.peek() and self.peek().type == TokenType.IDENTIFIER and self.peek().value == "|":
  1274→            self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "|")
  1275→            right = self.parse_and_expr()
  1276→            left = BinaryOpExpr(left, "|", right)
  1277→        return left
  1278→
  1279→    def parse_and_expr(self) -> Expression:
  1280→        left = self.parse_not_expr()
  1281→        while self.peek() and self.peek().type == TokenType.IDENTIFIER and self.peek().value == "&":
  1282→            self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "&")
  1283→            right = self.parse_not_expr()
  1284→            left = BinaryOpExpr(left, "&", right)
  1285→        return left
  1286→
  1287→    def parse_not_expr(self) -> Expression:
  1288→        if self.peek() and self.peek().type == TokenType.IDENTIFIER and self.peek().value == "!":
  1289→            self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "!")
  1290→            operand = self.parse_not_expr()
  1291→            return UnaryOpExpr("!", operand)
  1292→        return self.parse_comparison()
  1293→
  1294→    def parse_comparison(self) -> Expression:
  1295→        left = self.parse_additive()
  1296→
  1297→        if self.peek() and self.peek().type == TokenType.IDENTIFIER:
  1298→            op_val = self.peek().value
  1299→            if op_val in ("<", ">", "==", "!=", "<=", ">=", "in"):
  1300→                op = self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER).value
  1301→                right = self.parse_additive()
  1302→                return BinaryOpExpr(left, op, right)
  1303→
  1304→        return left
  1305→
  1306→    def parse_additive(self) -> Expression:
  1307→        left = self.parse_term()
  1308→        while self.peek() and self.peek().type == TokenType.IDENTIFIER:
  1309→            op = self.peek().value
  1310→            if op in ("+", "-"):
  1311→                self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
  1312→                right = self.parse_term()
  1313→                left = BinaryOpExpr(left, op, right)
  1314→            else:
  1315→                break
  1316→        return left
  1317→
  1318→    def parse_term(self) -> Expression:
  1319→        left = self.parse_factor()
  1320→        while self.peek() and self.peek().type == TokenType.IDENTIFIER:
  1321→            op = self.peek().value
  1322→            if op in ("*", "/", "**"):
  1323→                self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
  1324→                right = self.parse_factor()
  1325→                left = BinaryOpExpr(left, op, right)
  1326→            else:
  1327→                break
  1328→        return left
  1329→
  1330→    def parse_factor(self) -> Expression:
  1331→        if self.peek() is None:
  1332→            raise BadDSLException("Unexpected end of input")
  1333→
  1334→        token = self.peek()
  1335→
  1336→        # Parenthesized expression
  1337→        if token.type == TokenType.LPAREN:
  1338→            self.consume(TokenType.LPAREN)
  1339→            expr = self.parse_boolean_expr()
  1340→            self.consume(TokenType.RPAREN)
  1341→            return expr
  1342→
  1343→        # Function call: func(arg) or func(arg, arg)
  1344→        if token.type == TokenType.IDENTIFIER and self.peek_plus(1) and self.peek_plus(1).type == TokenType.LPAREN:
  1345→            func_name = self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER).value
  1346→            self.consume(TokenType.LPAREN)
  1347→
  1348→            # Check for special functions
  1349→            if func_name in ("lag", "lead"):
  1350→                arg = self.parse_expression()
  1351→                self.consume(TokenType.COMMA)
  1352→                k_expr = self.parse_expression()
  1353→                self.consume(TokenType.RPAREN)
  1354→                # Validate k is int
  1355→                if not (isinstance(k_expr, LiteralExpr) and isinstance(k_expr.type, IntType)):
  1356→                    raise TypeErrorException(f"{func_name}() requires integer k")
  1357→                return LagLeadExpr(func_name, arg, k_expr.value.value)
  1358→
  1359→            if func_name in ("starts_with", "ends_with", "contains"):
  1360→                arg1 = self.parse_expression()
  1361→                self.consume(TokenType.COMMA)
  1362→                arg2 = self.parse_expression()
  1363→                self.consume(TokenType.RPAREN)
  1364→                return BinaryStringFuncExpr(func_name, arg1, arg2)
  1365→
  1366→            if func_name == "coalesce":
  1367→                arg1 = self.parse_expression()
  1368→                self.consume(TokenType.COMMA)
  1369→                arg2 = self.parse_expression()
  1370→                self.consume(TokenType.RPAREN)
  1371→                return CoalesceExpr(arg1, arg2)
  1372→
  1373→            if func_name == "cast":
  1374→                arg = self.parse_expression()
  1375→                self.consume(TokenType.COMMA)
  1376→                type_token = self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
  1377→                type_str = type_token.value.lower()
  1378→                if type_str == "string":
  1379→                    target_type = StringType()
  1380→                elif type_str == "int":
  1381→                    target_type = IntType()
  1382→                elif type_str == "float":
  1383→                    target_type = NumberType()
  1384→                elif type_str == "bool":
  1385→                    target_type = BoolType()
  1386→                elif type_str == "timestamp":
  1387→                    target_type = TimestampType()
  1388→                else:
  1389→                    raise BadDSLException(f"Unknown type in cast: {type_str}")
  1390→                self.consume(TokenType.RBRACKET if self.peek().type == TokenType.RBRACKET else TokenType.RPAREN)
  1391→                # Check for AS keyword if present
  1392→                if self.peek() and self.peek().type == TokenType.IDENTIFIER and self.peek().value.lower() == "as":
  1393→                    self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)  # consume 'as'
  1394→                return CastExpr(arg, target_type)
  1395→
  1396→            # Regular transform function
  1397→            arg = self.parse_expression()
  1398→            digits = None
  1399→            if self.peek().type == TokenType.COMMA:
  1400→                self.consume(TokenType.COMMA)
  1401→                digits = self.parse_expression()
  1402→            self.consume(TokenType.RPAREN)
  1403→            return TransformExpr(func_name, arg, digits)
  1404→
  1405→        # param("name")
  1406→        if token.type == TokenType.IDENTIFIER and token.value == "param":
  1407→            self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)  # consume 'param'
  1408→            self.consume(TokenType.LPAREN)
  1409→            name_token = self.consume(TokenType.STRING_LITERAL)
  1410→            name = name_token.value
  1411→            self.consume(TokenType.RPAREN)
  1412→            return ParamRefExpr(name)
  1413→
  1414→        # Literal values
  1415→        if token.type == TokenType.STRING_LITERAL:
  1416→            self.consume()
  1417→            return LiteralExpr(MTLValue(token.value, StringType()))
  1418→
  1419→        if token.type == TokenType.NUMBER:
  1420→            self.consume()
  1421→            val = float(token.value)
  1422→            if val == int(val):
  1423→                return LiteralExpr(MTLValue(int(val), IntType()))
  1424→            return LiteralExpr(MTLValue(val, NumberType()))
  1425→
  1426→        if token.type == TokenType.BOOL:
  1427→            self.consume()
  1428→            return LiteralExpr(MTLValue(token.value.lower() == "true", BoolType()))
  1429→
  1430→        # Identifier (column reference)
  1431→        if token.type == TokenType.IDENTIFIER:
  1432→            name = self.consume(TokenType.IDENTIFIER).value
  1433→            return ColumnRefExpr(name)
  1434→
  1435→        raise BadDSLException(f"Unexpected token: {token.value} at line {token.line}")
  1436→
  1437→    def peek_plus(self, offset: int) -> Optional[Token]:
  1438→        if self.pos + offset < len(self.tokens):
  1439→            return self.tokens[self.pos + offset]
  1440→        return None
  1441→
  1442→    def parse_expression(self) -> Expression:
  1443→        """Parse a general expression."""
  1444→        return self.parse_boolean_expr()
  1445→
  1446→
  1447→def parse_dsl(dsl_text: str) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Schema], Pipeline]:
  1448→    """Parse DSL text into schemas and pipeline."""
  1449→    lexer = Lexer(dsl_text)
  1450→    tokens = lexer.tokenize()
  1451→
  1452→    # Filter out comments and whitespace for parsing
  1453→    clean_tokens = [t for t in tokens if t.type not in (TokenType.COMMENT, TokenType.WHITESPACE)]
  1454→
  1455→    parser = Parser(clean_tokens)
  1456→    return parser.parse()
  1457→
  1458→
  1459→# =============================================================================
  1460→# Data Processing Engine (Extended)
  1461→# =============================================================================
  1462→
  1463→@dataclass
  1464→class Row:
  1465→    """A row of data with typed values."""
  1466→    values: Dict[str, MTLValue]
  1467→    original_index: int = 0
  1468→
  1469→
  1470→class MTLEngine:
  1471→    def __init__(self, csv_path: str, dsl_path: str, tz: str = "UTC",
  1472→                 timestamp_format: Optional[str] = None,
  1473→                 cli_params: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None):
  1474→        self.tz = tz
  1475→        self.timestamp_format = timestamp_format
  1476→        self.resolved_schema: Optional[Schema] = None
  1477→        self.rows: List[Row] = []
  1478→        self.params: Dict[str, MTLValue] = {}
  1479→        self.pipeline: Optional[Pipeline] = None
  1480→
  1481→        # Parse DSL
  1482→        if dsl_path == "-":
  1483→            dsl_text = sys.stdin.read()
  1484→        else:
  1485→            try:
  1486→                with open(dsl_path, 'r') as f:
  1487→                    dsl_text = f.read()
  1488→            except IOError as e:
  1489→                raise BadIOException(f"Cannot read DSL file: {e}")
  1490→
  1491→        schemas, pipeline = parse_dsl(dsl_text)
  1492→        self.pipeline = pipeline
  1493→
  1494→        # Resolve schema inheritance
  1495→        schema_registry = SchemaRegistry()
  1496→        for schema in schemas.values():
  1497→            schema_registry.add_schema(schema)
  1498→
  1499→        if pipeline.schema_name not in schemas:
  1500→            raise BadDSLException(f"Schema '{pipeline.schema_name}' not found")
  1501→
  1502→        # Resolve with full inheritance
  1503→        self.resolved_schema = schema_registry.resolve_inheritance(pipeline.schema_name)
  1504→
  1505→        # Process parameters
  1506→        self._process_parameters(pipeline, cli_params or {})
  1507→
  1508→        # Validate pipeline uses resolved schema
  1509→        self._validate_pipeline()
  1510→
  1511→        # Load CSV
  1512→        self._load_csv(csv_path)
  1513→
  1514→    def _process_parameters(self, pipeline: Pipeline, cli_params: Dict[str, str]):
  1515→        """Process and validate pipeline parameters."""
  1516→        # First, collect provided params from CLI
  1517→        provided_from_cli: Dict[str, MTLValue] = {}
  1518→
  1519→        for name, value_str in cli_params.items():
  1520→            if name not in pipeline.params:
  1521→                raise BadDSLException(f"Unknown parameter: {name}")
  1522→
  1523→            param = pipeline.params[name]
  1524→            try:
  1525→                if isinstance(param.type, StringType):
  1526→                    provided_from_cli[name] = MTLValue(value_str, StringType())
  1527→                elif isinstance(param.type, IntType):
  1528→                    provided_from_cli[name] = MTLValue(int(value_str), IntType())
  1529→                elif isinstance(param.type, NumberType):
  1530→                    provided_from_cli[name] = MTLValue(float(value_str), NumberType())
  1531→                elif isinstance(param.type, BoolType):
  1532→                    provided_from_cli[name] = MTLValue(value_str.lower() in ("true", "1", "yes"), BoolType())
  1533→                else:
  1534→                    raise TypeErrorException(f"Cannot parse value for parameter {name}")
  1535→            except ValueError:
  1536→                raise TypeErrorException(f"Cannot parse '{value_str}' as {type(param.type).__name__} for parameter {name}")
  1537→
  1538→        # Check for required params without defaults
  1539→        for name, param in pipeline.params.items():
  1540→            if name in provided_from_cli:
  1541→                self.params[name] = provided_from_cli[name]
  1542→            elif param.default is not None:
  1543→                self.params[name] = param.default
  1544→            else:
  1545→                raise BadDSLException(f"Parameter '{name}' must be provided via --param")
  1546→
  1547→    def _validate_pipeline(self):
  1548→        """Validate pipeline references and types."""
  1549→        schema = self.resolved_schema
  1550→
  1551→        # Validate group_by columns exist
  1552→        for col in self.pipeline.group_by_cols:
  1553→            if col not in schema.fields:
  1554→                # Check if it's a calculated field
  1555→                calc_field = next((c for c in self.pipeline.calc_stmts if c.stage == "pre" and c.name == col), None)
  1556→                if calc_field is None:
  1557→                    raise BadDSLException(f"Unknown column in group_by: {col}")
  1558→
  1559→        # Validate calc statements
  1560→        pre_calc_names = set()
  1561→        post_calc_names = set()
  1562→
  1563→        for calc in self.pipeline.calc_stmts:
  1564→            if calc.stage == "pre":
  1565→                if calc.name in pre_calc_names:
  1566→                    raise BadDSLException(f"Duplicate calc name: {calc.name}")
  1567→                pre_calc_names.add(calc.name)
  1568→            else:  # post_agg
  1569→                if calc.name in post_calc_names:
  1570→                    raise BadDSLException(f"Duplicate calc name: {calc.name}")
  1571→                post_calc_names.add(calc.name)
  1572→
  1573→            # Validate expression type matches declared type
  1574→            try:
  1575→                expr_type = calc.expr.get_type(schema.fields)
  1576→                if not types_compatible(expr_type, calc.type):
  1577→                    raise TypeErrorException(
  1578→                        f"Calc '{calc.name}' type mismatch: expression has {type(expr_type).__name__}, "
  1579→                        f"declared as {type(calc.type).__name__}"
  1580→                    )
  1581→            except Exception as e:
  1582→                if isinstance(e, (BadDSLException, TypeErrorException)):
  1583→                    raise
  1584→                raise TypeErrorException(f"Calc '{calc.name}' type error: {e}")
  1585→
  1586→        # Validate post_agg calcs don't reference pre-stage-only constructs incorrectly
  1587→        for calc in self.pipeline.calc_stmts:
  1588→            if calc.stage == "post_agg":
  1589→                # Check for aggregate aliases
  1590→                if self.pipeline.aggregate_spec:
  1591→                    for agg in self.pipeline.aggregate_spec["aggs"]:
  1592→                        if agg["alias"] in get_expr_vars(calc.expr):
  1593→                            # This is OK - post_agg can reference aggregates
  1594→                            pass
  1595→
  1596→    def _load_csv(self, csv_path: str):
  1597→        """Load CSV and create rows with schema-applied types."""
  1598→        try:
  1599→            with open(csv_path, 'r', newline='') as f:
  1600→                reader = csv.DictReader(f)
  1601→                if reader.fieldnames is None:
  1602→                    raise BadIOException("CSV file has no header")
  1603→
  1604→                # Get schema fields - these are the expected physical columns
  1605→                schema_fields = {name: field for name, field in self.resolved_schema.fields.items()
  1606→                                if not field.is_calculated}
  1607→
  1608→                # Read all rows
  1609→                row_idx = 0
  1610→                for row_dict in reader:
  1611→                    row_values = {}
  1612→
  1613→                    # Apply schema types to CSV data
  1614→                    for col, field in schema_fields.items():
  1615→                        val_str = row_dict.get(col, "")
  1616→                        row_values[col] = self._parse_value(field.type, val_str, col)
  1617→
  1618→                    # Add calculated fields from schema (pre-stage)
  1619→                    for field_name, field in self.resolved_schema.fields.items():
  1620→                        if field.is_calculated and field.expr:
  1621→                            try:
  1622→                                result = field.expr.eval(row_values)
  1623→                                row_values[field_name] = result
  1624→                            except Exception as e:
  1625→                                raise BadDSLException(f"Schema calc '{field_name}' failed: {e}")
  1626→
  1627→                    self.rows.append(Row(row_values, row_idx))
  1628→                    row_idx += 1
  1629→
  1630→        except FileNotFoundError:
  1631→            raise BadIOException(f"CSV file not found: {csv_path}")
  1632→        except IOError as e:
  1633→            raise BadIOException(f"Cannot read CSV file: {e}")
  1634→
  1635→    def _parse_value(self, col_type: ValueType, val_str: str, col_name: str) -> MTLValue:
  1636→        """Parse a string value according to its schema type."""
  1637→        if val_str == "" or val_str is None:
  1638→            # Return null-like value
  1639→            return MTLValue(None, col_type)
  1640→
  1641→        if isinstance(col_type, TimestampType):
  1642→            return self._parse_timestamp(val_str)
  1643→        elif isinstance(col_type, (IntType, NumberType)):
  1644→            try:
  1645→                if '.' in val_str or 'e' in val_str.lower():
  1646→                    return MTLValue(float(val_str), NumberType())
  1647→                else:
  1648→                    return MTLValue(int(val_str), IntType())
  1649→            except ValueError:
  1650→                raise TypeErrorException(f"Cannot parse '{val_str}' as number for column {col_name}")
  1651→        else:
  1652→            return MTLValue(val_str, StringType())
  1653→
  1654→    def _parse_timestamp(self, ts_str: str) -> MTLValue:
  1655→        """Parse a timestamp string."""
  1656→        try:
  1657→            if 'T' in ts_str or (ts_str.count('-') == 2 and ts_str.count(':') >= 2):
  1658→                if '+' in ts_str or (ts_str.count('-') > 2):
  1659→                    ts_str = ts_str.replace('Z', '+00:00')
  1660→                    dt = datetime.fromisoformat(ts_str.replace('+00:00', '+00:00'))
  1661→                else:
  1662→                    dt = datetime.fromisoformat(ts_str)
  1663→                    if dt.tzinfo is None:
  1664→                        dt = dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
  1665→            else:
  1666→                if self.timestamp_format:
  1667→                    dt = datetime.strptime(ts_str, self.timestamp_format)
  1668→                else:
  1669→                    dt = datetime.fromisoformat(ts_str)
  1670→                if dt.tzinfo is None:
  1671→                    from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo
  1672→                    tz_obj = ZoneInfo(self.tz)
  1673→                    dt = dt.replace(tzinfo=tz_obj)
  1674→
  1675→            return MTLValue(dt, TimestampType())
  1676→        except Exception as e:
  1677→            raise TypeErrorException(f"Cannot parse timestamp '{ts_str}': {e}")
  1678→
  1679→    def execute(self) -> Any:
  1680→        """Execute the transformation pipeline."""
  1681→        pipeline = self.pipeline
  1682→        schema = self.resolved_schema
  1683→
  1684→        # Build the full schema with pre-calc fields
  1685→        current_schema = dict(schema.fields)
  1686→
  1687→        # Apply pre-stage calcs
  1688→        current_rows = self.rows
  1689→        for calc in pipeline.calc_stmts:
  1690→            if calc.stage == "pre":
  1691→                # Add calc to schema
  1692→                current_schema[calc.name] = SchemaField(calc.name, calc.type, calc.expr, True)
  1693→
  1694→        # Apply filter
  1695→        if pipeline.filter_expr:
  1696→            current_rows = self._apply_filter(current_rows, pipeline.filter_expr, current_schema)
  1697→
  1698→        # Apply apply statements
  1699→        for expr, new_col in pipeline.apply_stmts:
  1700→            current_rows = self._apply_apply(current_rows, expr, new_col, current_schema)
  1701→            current_schema[new_col] = SchemaField(new_col, expr.get_type(current_schema), None, False)
  1702→
  1703→        # Apply windowing
  1704→        if pipeline.window_duration:
  1705→            current_rows = self._apply_window(current_rows, pipeline.window_duration)
  1706→            current_schema["window_start"] = SchemaField("window_start", TimestampType(), None, False)
  1707→            current_schema["window_end"] = SchemaField("window_end", TimestampType(), None, False)
  1708→
  1709→        # Apply grouping and aggregation
  1710→        if not pipeline.aggregate_spec:
  1711→            raise BadDSLException("Missing required aggregate statement")
  1712→
  1713→        results = self._apply_aggregation(
  1714→            current_rows, pipeline.aggregate_spec,
  1715→            pipeline.group_by_cols, pipeline.window_duration, current_schema
  1716→        )
  1717→
  1718→        # Apply post-aggregation calcs
  1719→        post_calc_stmts = [c for c in pipeline.calc_stmts if c.stage == "post_agg"]
  1720→        if post_calc_stmts:
  1721→            results = self._apply_post_agg_calcs(results, post_calc_stmts, pipeline.group_by_cols)
  1722→
  1723→        # Sort results
  1724→        results = self._sort_results(results, pipeline.group_by_cols, pipeline.window_duration)
  1725→
  1726→        return results
  1727→
  1728→    def _apply_filter(self, rows: List[Row], expr: Expression, schema: Dict[str, SchemaField]) -> List[Row]:
  1729→        """Filter rows based on expression."""
  1730→        filtered = []
  1731→        for row in rows:
  1732→            try:
  1733→                # Resolve param references
  1734→                context = self._resolve_expr_params(row.values, expr)
  1735→                result = expr.eval(context)
  1736→                if result.as_bool():
  1737→                    filtered.append(row)
  1738→            except RuntimeException:
  1739→                raise
  1740→            except Exception as e:
  1741→                raise TypeErrorException(f"Filter evaluation error: {e}")
  1742→        return filtered
  1743→
  1744→    def _apply_apply(self, rows: List[Row], expr: Expression, new_col: str,
  1745→                     schema: Dict[str, SchemaField]) -> List[Row]:
  1746→        """Apply transform and add new column."""
  1747→        for row in rows:
  1748→            try:
  1749→                context = self._resolve_expr_params(row.values, expr)
  1750→                result = expr.eval(context)
  1751→                row.values[new_col] = result
  1752→            except RuntimeException:
  1753→                raise
  1754→            except Exception as e:
  1755→                raise TypeErrorException(f"Apply evaluation error: {e}")
  1756→        return rows
  1757→
  1758→    def _resolve_expr_params(self, values: Dict[str, MTLValue], expr: Expression) -> Dict[str, MTLValue]:
  1759→        """Resolve param references in an expression to actual values."""
  1760→        # For now, we'll handle params during evaluation
  1761→        # The expression should be evaluated with params resolved
  1762→        return values
  1763→
  1764→    def _apply_apply_with_params(self, rows: List[Row], expr: Expression, new_col: str,
  1765→                                  schema: Dict[str, SchemaField]) -> List[Row]:
  1766→        """Apply transform with parameter resolution."""
  1767→        for row in rows:
  1768→            try:
  1769→                # Replace param references with actual values
  1770→                resolved_expr = self._resolve_params_in_expr(expr)
  1771→                result = resolved_expr.eval(row.values)
  1772→                row.values[new_col] = result
  1773→            except RuntimeException:
  1774→                raise
  1775→            except Exception as e:
  1776→                raise TypeErrorException(f"Apply evaluation error: {e}")
  1777→        return rows
  1778→
  1779→    def _resolve_params_in_expr(self, expr: Expression) -> Expression:
  1780→        """Replace ParamRefExpr with literal values."""
  1781→        if isinstance(expr, ParamRefExpr):
  1782→            if expr.name not in self.params:
  1783→                raise RuntimeException(f"Parameter '{expr.name}' not provided")
  1784→            return LiteralExpr(self.params[expr.name])
  1785→
  1786→        if isinstance(expr, BinaryOpExpr):
  1787→            return BinaryOpExpr(
  1788→                self._resolve_params_in_expr(expr.left),
  1789→                expr.op,
  1790→                self._resolve_params_in_expr(expr.right)
  1791→            )
  1792→
  1793→        if isinstance(expr, UnaryOpExpr):
  1794→            return UnaryOpExpr(expr.op, self._resolve_params_in_expr(expr.operand))
  1795→
  1796→        if isinstance(expr, TransformExpr):
  1797→            return TransformExpr(
  1798→                expr.func,
  1799→                self._resolve_params_in_expr(expr.arg),
  1800→                self._resolve_params_in_expr(expr.digits) if expr.digits else None
  1801→            )
  1802→
  1803→        if isinstance(expr, CoalesceExpr):
  1804→            return CoalesceExpr(
  1805→                self._resolve_params_in_expr(expr.left),
  1806→                self._resolve_params_in_expr(expr.right)
  1807→            )
  1808→
  1809→        if isinstance(expr, CastExpr):
  1810→            return CastExpr(
  1811→                self._resolve_params_in_expr(expr.expr),
  1812→                expr.target_type
  1813→            )
  1814→
  1815→        if isinstance(expr, BinaryStringFuncExpr):
  1816→            return BinaryStringFuncExpr(
  1817→                expr.func,
  1818→                self._resolve_params_in_expr(expr.left),
  1819→                self._resolve_params_in_expr(expr.right)
  1820→            )
  1821→
  1822→        return expr
  1823→
  1824→    def _apply_window(self, rows: List[Row], duration: str) -> List[Row]:
  1825→        """Add window_start and window_end columns."""
  1826→        match = self._parse_duration(duration)
  1827→        unit_multipliers = {"m": 60, "h": 3600, "d": 86400}
  1828→        duration_seconds = match[0] * unit_multipliers[match[1]]
  1829→
  1830→        epoch = datetime(1970, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
  1831→
  1832→        for row in rows:
  1833→            ts_val = row.values.get("timestamp")
  1834→            if ts_val is None or ts_val.value is None:
  1835→                row.values["window_start"] = MTLValue(None, TimestampType())
  1836→                row.values["window_end"] = MTLValue(None, TimestampType())
  1837→                continue
  1838→
  1839→            ts = ts_val.as_timestamp()
  1840→            if ts.tzinfo is None:
  1841→                from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo
  1842→                tz_obj = ZoneInfo(self.tz)
  1843→                ts = ts.replace(tzinfo=tz_obj)
  1844→            ts_utc = ts.astimezone(timezone.utc)
  1845→
  1846→            diff_seconds = (ts_utc - epoch).total_seconds()
  1847→            window_start_seconds = int(diff_seconds // duration_seconds) * duration_seconds
  1848→            window_start = epoch + timedelta(seconds=window_start_seconds)
  1849→            window_end = window_start + timedelta(seconds=duration_seconds)
  1850→
  1851→            row.values["window_start"] = MTLValue(window_start, TimestampType())
  1852→            row.values["window_end"] = MTLValue(window_end, TimestampType())
  1853→
  1854→        return rows
  1855→
  1856→    def _parse_duration(self, duration: str) -> Tuple[int, str]:
  1857→        """Parse duration literal like '5m', '1h', '7d'."""
  1858→        if duration.endswith('m'):
  1859→            return int(duration[:-1]), 'm'
  1860→        elif duration.endswith('h'):
  1861→            return int(duration[:-1]), 'h'
  1862→        elif duration.endswith('d'):
  1863→            return int(duration[:-1]), 'd'
  1864→        else:
  1865→            raise BadDSLException(f"Invalid duration literal: {duration}")
  1866→
  1867→    def _apply_aggregation(self, rows: List[Row], aggregate_spec: Dict,
  1868→                           group_by_cols: List[str], window_duration: Optional[str],
  1869→                           schema: Dict[str, SchemaField]) -> List[Dict]:
  1870→        """Apply aggregation and return result."""
  1871→        if not rows:
  1872→            if not group_by_cols and not window_duration:
  1873→                return [self._build_empty_aggregates(aggregate_spec)]
  1874→            else:
  1875→                return []
  1876→
  1877→        groups: Dict[Tuple, List[Row]] = {}
  1878→
  1879→        if group_by_cols:
  1880→            for row in rows:
  1881→                key_values = []
  1882→                for col in group_by_cols:
  1883→                    val = row.values.get(col)
  1884→                    if val is not None and val.value is not None:
  1885→                        key_values.append(val.value)
  1886→                    else:
  1887→                        key_values.append(None)
  1888→                key = tuple(key_values)
  1889→                if key not in groups:
  1890→                    groups[key] = []
  1891→                groups[key].append(row)
  1892→        else:
  1893→            groups[()] = rows
  1894→
  1895→        results = []
  1896→
  1897→        for key, group_rows in groups.items():
  1898→            result_obj: Dict[str, Any] = {}
  1899→
  1900→            # Add group-by keys
  1901→            if group_by_cols:
  1902→                for i, col in enumerate(group_by_cols):
  1903→                    val = key[i]
  1904→                    if val is not None:
  1905→                        result_obj[col] = val
  1906→                    else:
  1907→                        result_obj[col] = None
  1908→
  1909→            # Add window keys
  1910→            if window_duration:
  1911→                ws = group_rows[0].values.get("window_start")
  1912→                we = group_rows[0].values.get("window_end")
  1913→                if ws and ws.value:
  1914→                    result_obj["window_start"] = ws.value.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
  1915→                else:
  1916→                    result_obj["window_start"] = None
  1917→                if we and we.value:
  1918→                    result_obj["window_end"] = we.value.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
  1919→                else:
  1920→                    result_obj["window_end"] = None
  1921→
  1922→            # Compute aggregates
  1923→            for agg in aggregate_spec["aggs"]:
  1924→                alias = agg["alias"]
  1925→                func = agg["func"]
  1926→                arg_expr = agg["arg"]
  1927→
  1928→                if isinstance(arg_expr, LiteralExpr) and arg_expr.value.value == "*":
  1929→                    if func == "count":
  1930→                        result_obj[alias] = len(group_rows)
  1931→                    else:
  1932→                        raise TypeErrorException(f"Aggregate {func}(*) is not valid")
  1933→                else:
  1934→                    col_expr = arg_expr
  1935→
  1936→                    if isinstance(col_expr, ColumnRefExpr):
  1937→                        col_name = col_expr.name
  1938→                    else:
  1939→                        col_name = getattr(col_expr, 'name', str(col_expr))
  1940→
  1941→                    values = []
  1942→                    for row in group_rows:
  1943→                        if col_name in row.values:
  1944→                            val = row.values[col_name]
  1945→                            if val and val.is_numeric():
  1946→                                values.append(val.as_number())
  1947→
  1948→                    if not values:
  1949→                        if func == "count":
  1950→                            result_obj[alias] = 0
  1951→                        else:
  1952→                            result_obj[alias] = None
  1953→                    else:
  1954→                        result_obj[alias] = self._compute_aggregate(func, values)
  1955→
  1956→            results.append(result_obj)
  1957→
  1958→        return results
  1959→
  1960→    def _compute_aggregate(self, func: str, values: List[float]) -> Any:
  1961→        """Compute aggregate function on values."""
  1962→        n = len(values)
  1963→
  1964→        if func == "sum":
  1965→            return sum(values)
  1966→        elif func == "average":
  1967→            return sum(values) / n
  1968→        elif func == "median":
  1969→            sorted_vals = sorted(values)
  1970→            mid = n // 2
  1971→            if n % 2 == 1:
  1972→                return sorted_vals[mid]
  1973→            else:
  1974→                return (sorted_vals[mid - 1] + sorted_vals[mid]) / 2
  1975→        elif func == "count":
  1976→            return n
  1977→        elif func == "std":
  1978→            if n == 0:
  1979→                return None
  1980→            mean = sum(values) / n
  1981→            variance = sum((x - mean) ** 2 for x in values) / n
  1982→            return math.sqrt(variance)
  1983→        elif func == "var":
  1984→            if n == 0:
  1985→                return None
  1986→            mean = sum(values) / n
  1987→            return sum((x - mean) ** 2 for x in values) / n
  1988→        elif func == "min":
  1989→            return min(values)
  1990→        elif func == "max":
  1991→            return max(values)
  1992→        else:
  1993→            raise TypeErrorException(f"Unknown aggregate function: {func}")
  1994→
  1995→    def _build_empty_aggregates(self, aggregate_spec: Dict) -> Dict:
  1996→        """Build empty aggregate result."""
  1997→        result = {}
  1998→        for agg in aggregate_spec["aggs"]:

  2000→            func = agg["func"]
  2001→            if func == "count":
  2002→                result[alias] = 0
  2003→            else:
  2004→                result[alias] = None
  2005→        return result

  2007→    def _apply_post_agg_calcs(self, results: List[Dict], calc_stmts: List[CalcStatement],
  2008→                               group_by_cols: List[str]) -> List[Dict]:
  2009→        """Apply post-aggregation calculated fields."""
  2010→        # First, create a wrapper that includes lag/lead support

  2014→            # Create a context with lag/lead support
  2015→            context = {}
  2016→
  2017→            # Add all result keys to context

  2019→                if isinstance(val, (int, float)):

  2023→                elif val is None:

  2025→
  2026→            # Add lag/lead metadata
  2027→            context["_laglead_index"] = MTLValue(idx, IntType())
  2028→            context["_laglead_results"] = MTLValue(results, StringType())  # Pass results array
  2029→
  2030→            # Evaluate each calc

  2034→                    result_val = resolved_expr.eval(context)
  2035→                    result[calc.name] = result_val.value
  2036→                except Exception as e:
  2037→                    raise RuntimeException(f"Post-agg calc '{calc.name}' failed: {e}")
  2038→
  2039→        return results
  2040→
  2041→    def _sort_results(self, results: List[Dict], group_by_cols: List[str],
  2042→                      window_duration: Optional[str]) -> List[Dict]:
  2043→        """Sort results according to normalization rules."""
  2044→
  2045→        def sort_key(item: Dict) -> Tuple:
  2046→            key_parts = []
  2047→
  2048→            if window_duration:
  2049→                ws = item.get("window_start", "")
  2050→                key_parts.append(ws if ws is not None else "")
  2051→
  2052→            if group_by_cols:
  2053→                for col in group_by_cols:
  2054→                    val = item.get(col)
  2055→                    if val is None:
  2056→                        key_parts.append((1, ""))
  2057→                    elif isinstance(val, str):
  2058→                        key_parts.append((0, val))
  2059→                    elif isinstance(val, (int, float)):
  2060→                        key_parts.append((2, val))
  2061→                    else:
  2062→                        key_parts.append((3, str(val)))
  2063→
  2064→            return tuple(key_parts)
  2065→
  2066→        return sorted(results, key=sort_key)

  2068→
  2069→def get_expr_vars(expr: Expression) -> Set[str]:
  2070→    """Extract variable names referenced in an expression."""
  2071→    vars: Set[str] = set()
  2072→
  2073→    if isinstance(expr, ColumnRefExpr):
  2074→        vars.add(expr.name)
  2075→    elif isinstance(expr, BinaryOpExpr):
  2076→        vars.update(get_expr_vars(expr.left))
  2077→        vars.update(get_expr_vars(expr.right))
  2078→    elif isinstance(expr, UnaryOpExpr):
  2079→        vars.update(get_expr_vars(expr.operand))
  2080→    elif isinstance(expr, TransformExpr):
  2081→        vars.update(get_expr_vars(expr.arg))
  2082→        if expr.digits:
  2083→            vars.update(get_expr_vars(expr.digits))
  2084→    elif isinstance(expr, CoalesceExpr):
  2085→        vars.update(get_expr_vars(expr.left))
  2086→        vars.update(get_expr_vars(expr.right))
  2087→    elif isinstance(expr, CastExpr):
  2088→        vars.update(get_expr_vars(expr.expr))
  2089→    elif isinstance(expr, BinaryStringFuncExpr):
  2090→        vars.update(get_expr_vars(expr.left))
  2091→        vars.update(get_expr_vars(expr.right))
  2092→
  2093→    return vars

  2121→        key, value = param_str.split('=', 1)

  2123→
  2124→    try:

  2129→            timestamp_format=args.timestamp_format,
  2130→            cli_params=cli_params

  2132→
  2133→        result = engine.execute()
  2134→
  2135→        # Output
  2136→        output_json = json.dumps(result, indent=2)
  2137→
  2138→        if args.out:

  2141→        else:

  2143→
  2144→        sys.exit(0)
  2145→
  2146→    except MTLException as e:
  2147→        print(e.stderr_message(), file=sys.stderr)
  2148→        sys.exit(e.exit_code)
  2149→    except Exception as e:
  2150→        print(f"ERROR:unexpected:{e}", file=sys.stderr)
  2151→        sys.exit(1)
  2152→

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Test suite for MTL extended functionality.
"""

import subprocess
import json
import sys
import tempfile
import os

def run_mtl(csv_content, dsl_content, params=None):
    """Run MTL with given CSV and DSL content."""
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(mode='w', suffix='.csv', delete=False) as f:
        f.write(csv_content)
        csv_path = f.name

    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(mode='w', suffix='.mtl', delete=False) as f:
        f.write(dsl_content)
        dsl_path = f.name

    cmd = ['python', 'mtl.py', '--csv', csv_path, '--dsl', dsl_path]
    if params:
        for key, value in params.items():
            cmd.extend(['--param', f'{key}={value}'])

    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)

    os.unlink(csv_path)
    os.unlink(dsl_path)

    return result


def test_basic_part1_compatibility():
    """Test that Part 1 functionality still works."""
    print("Testing Part 1 compatibility...")

    csv_content = """timestamp,location,price,quantity
2024-01-01T10:00:00Z,New York,10.0,2
2024-01-01T11:00:00Z,Boston,20.0,3
2024-01-01T12:00:00Z,New York,15.0,1
"""

    dsl_content = """
    filter(location == "New York")
    window(1h)
    group_by([location])
    aggregate sum(price) as total_price
    """

    result = run_mtl(csv_content, dsl_content)
    assert result.returncode == 0, f"Failed: {result.stderr}"

    output = json.loads(result.stdout)
    assert len(output) == 1
    assert output[0]['location'] == 'New York'
    assert output[0]['total_price'] == 25.0  # 10*2 + 15*1
    print("✓ Part 1 compatibility works")


def test_schema_with_inheritance():
    """Test schema definition with inheritance."""
    print("\nTesting schema with inheritance...")

    csv_content = """timestamp,location,item,price,quantity,unit,category,promo_code
2024-01-01T10:00:00Z,Store A,Widget,10.0,2,pcs,electronics,SUMMER2024
2024-01-01T11:00:00Z,Store B,Gadget,20.0,1,pcs,electronics,SPRING2024
2024-01-01T12:00:00Z,Store A,Thing,15.0,3,pcs,other,SUMMER2024
"""

    dsl_content = """
    schema BaseSale {
        timestamp: timestamp
        location: string
        item: string
        price: float
        quantity: float
        unit: string
        category: string
    }

    schema PromoSale extends BaseSale {
        quantity: float
        promo_code: string
        revenue: float = price * quantity
    }

    pipeline TestPipeline using PromoSale {
        filter(promo_code == "SUMMER2024")
        window(1h)
        group_by([location])
        aggregate sum(revenue) as total_revenue,
                  count(*) as n
    }
    """

    result = run_mtl(csv_content, dsl_content)
    assert result.returncode == 0, f"Failed: {result.stderr}"

    output = json.loads(result.stdout)
    assert len(output) == 2  # Store A appears twice

    # Find Store A result
    store_a = next(o for o in output if o['location'] == 'Store A')
    assert store_a['total_revenue'] == 65.0  # (10*2) + (15*3)
    print("✓ Schema inheritance works")


def test_type_override_widening():
    """Test that numeric widening (int->float) is allowed."""
    print("\nTesting type override widening...")

    csv_content = """timestamp,location,item,price,quantity
2024-01-01T10:00:00Z,Store A,Widget,10.0,2
2024-01-01T11:00:00Z,Store B,Gadget,20.0,3
"""

    dsl_content = """
    schema BaseSale {
        timestamp: timestamp
        location: string
        item: string
        price: float
        quantity: int
    }

    schema ExtendedSale extends BaseSale {
        quantity: float  # int -> float widening allowed
    }

    pipeline TestPipeline using ExtendedSale {
        aggregate sum(quantity) as total_qty
    }
    """

    result = run_mtl(csv_content, dsl_content)
    assert result.returncode == 0, f"Failed: {result.stderr}"
    print("✓ Type override widening works")


def test_illegal_type_override():
    """Test that illegal type overrides are rejected."""
    print("\nTesting illegal type override rejection...")

    csv_content = """timestamp,location,item,price,quantity
2024-01-01T10:00:00Z,Store A,Widget,10.0,2
"""

    dsl_content = """
    schema BaseSale {
        timestamp: timestamp
        location: string
        item: string
        price: float
        quantity: int
    }

    schema ExtendedSale extends BaseSale {
        quantity: string  # illegal: float -> string
    }

    pipeline TestPipeline using ExtendedSale {
        aggregate sum(quantity) as total_qty
    }
    """

    result = run_mtl(csv_content, dsl_content)
    assert result.returncode == 2, f"Should have failed with type error, got: {result.stderr}"
    assert "type_error" in result.stderr
    print("✓ Illegal type override correctly rejected")


def test_pipeline_parameters():
    """Test pipeline parameters."""
    print("\nTesting pipeline parameters...")

    csv_content = """timestamp,location,price,quantity
2024-01-01T10:00:00Z,Store A,10.0,2
2024-01-01T11:00:00Z,Store B,20.0,3
2024-01-01T12:00:00Z,Store C,5.0,1
"""

    dsl_content = """
    schema Sale {
        timestamp: timestamp
        location: string
        price: float
        quantity: float
    }

    pipeline FilteredPipeline using Sale {
        params {
            min_price: float = 0.0
        }

        filter(price >= param("min_price"))
        aggregate sum(price) as total_price
    }
    """

    # Test with parameter
    result = run_mtl(csv_content, dsl_content, params={'min_price': '15.0'})
    assert result.returncode == 0, f"Failed: {result.stderr}"

    output = json.loads(result.stdout)
    # Only rows with price >= 15: Store B (20), Store C is below
    # Actually all rows pass since min is 15 and we have 20 and 5
    # Let me check: 10 < 15, 20 >= 15, 5 < 15
    # So only Store B should remain
    assert len(output) == 1
    assert output[0]['total_price'] == 20.0
    print("✓ Pipeline parameters work")


def test_pre_stage_calculated_fields():
    """Test pre-stage calculated fields in pipeline."""
    print("\nTesting pre-stage calculated fields...")

    csv_content = """timestamp,location,price,quantity
2024-01-01T10:00:00Z,Store A,10.0,2
2024-01-01T11:00:00Z,Store B,20.0,3
"""

    dsl_content = """
    schema Sale {
        timestamp: timestamp
        location: string
        price: float
        quantity: float
    }

    pipeline TestPipeline using Sale {
        calc revenue = price * quantity : float @stage(pre)
        filter(revenue >= 30.0)
        group_by([location])
        aggregate sum(revenue) as total_revenue
    }
    """

    result = run_mtl(csv_content, dsl_content)
    assert result.returncode == 0, f"Failed: {result.stderr}"

    output = json.loads(result.stdout)
    # Store A: 10*2 = 20 (filtered out)
    # Store B: 20*3 = 60 (kept)
    assert len(output) == 1
    assert output[0]['total_revenue'] == 60.0
    print("✓ Pre-stage calculated fields work")


def test_post_agg_calculated_fields():
    """Test post-aggregation calculated fields."""
    print("\nTesting post-aggregation calculated fields...")

    csv_content = """timestamp,location,price,quantity
2024-01-01T10:00:00Z,Store A,10.0,2
2024-01-01T11:00:00Z,Store B,20.0,3
2024-01-01T12:00:00Z,Store A,15.0,1
2024-01-01T13:00:00Z,Store B,25.0,2
"""

    dsl_content = """
    schema Sale {
        timestamp: timestamp
        location: string
        price: float
        quantity: float
    }

    pipeline TestPipeline using Sale {
        group_by([location])
        aggregate sum(price * quantity) as revenue,
                  count(*) as transaction_count
        calc avg_per_tx = revenue / transaction_count : float @stage(post_agg)
    }
    """

    result = run_mtl(csv_content, dsl_content)
    assert result.returncode == 0, f"Failed: {result.stderr}"

    output = json.loads(result.stdout)
    assert len(output) == 2

    # Store A: (10*2 + 15*1) / 2 = 35/2 = 17.5
    # Store B: (20*3 + 25*2) / 2 = (60+50)/2 = 55
    store_a = next(o for o in output if o['location'] == 'Store A')
    store_b = next(o for o in output if o['location'] == 'Store B')

    assert abs(store_a['avg_per_tx'] - 17.5) < 0.001
    assert abs(store_b['avg_per_tx'] - 55.0) < 0.001
    print("✓ Post-aggregation calculated fields work")


def test_lag_function():
    """Test LAG function in post-aggregation."""
    print("\nTesting LAG function...")

    csv_content = """timestamp,location,price,quantity
2024-01-01T10:00:00Z,Store A,10.0,1
2024-01-01T11:00:00Z,Store B,20.0,1
2024-01-01T12:00:00Z,Store A,30.0,1
2024-01-01T13:00:00Z,Store B,40.0,1
"""

    dsl_content = """
    schema Sale {
        timestamp: timestamp
        location: string
        price: float
        quantity: float
    }

    pipeline TestPipeline using Sale {
        group_by([location])
        aggregate sum(price) as total_price
        calc prev_price = lag(total_price, 1) : float @stage(post_agg)
    }
    """

    result = run_mtl(csv_content, dsl_content)
    assert result.returncode == 0, f"Failed: {result.stderr}"

    output = json.loads(result.stdout)
    assert len(output) == 2

    # Results are sorted by location
    # Store A: total_price = 10+30 = 40, prev_price = null (first)
    # Store B: total_price = 20+40 = 60, prev_price = 40
    store_a = next(o for o in output if o['location'] == 'Store A')
    store_b = next(o for o in output if o['location'] == 'Store B')

    assert store_a['total_price'] == 40.0
    assert store_a['prev_price'] is None  # First in sorted order

    assert store_b['total_price'] == 60.0
    assert store_b['prev_price'] == 40.0
    print("✓ LAG function works")


def test_coalesce_function():
    """Test coalesce function."""
    print("\nTesting coalesce function...")

    csv_content = """timestamp,location,price,quantity
2024-01-01T10:00:00Z,Store A,10.0,2
2024-01-01T11:00:00Z,Store B,0.0,3
"""

    dsl_content = """
    schema Sale {
        timestamp: timestamp
        location: string
        price: float
        quantity: float
    }

    pipeline TestPipeline using Sale {
        calc safe_price = coalesce(price, 5.0) : float @stage(pre)
        group_by([location])
        aggregate sum(safe_price) as total_price
    }
    """

    result = run_mtl(csv_content, dsl_content)
    assert result.returncode == 0, f"Failed: {result.stderr}"

    output = json.loads(result.stdout)
    assert len(output) == 2

    store_a = next(o for o in output if o['location'] == 'Store A')
    store_b = next(o for o in output if o['location'] == 'Store B')

    assert store_a['total_price'] == 10.0
    assert store_b['total_price'] == 0.0  # 0.0 is not None, so price is used
    print("✓ Coalesce function works")


def test_schema_calculated_field():
    """Test calculated fields defined in schema."""
    print("\nTesting schema-level calculated fields...")

    csv_content = """timestamp,location,price,quantity
2024-01-01T10:00:00Z,Store A,10.0,2
2024-01-01T11:00:00Z,Store B,20.0,3
"""

    dsl_content = """
    schema Sale {
        timestamp: timestamp
        location: string
        price: float
        quantity: float
        revenue: float = price * quantity
    }

    pipeline TestPipeline using Sale {
        aggregate sum(revenue) as total_revenue
    }
    """

    result = run_mtl(csv_content, dsl_content)
    assert result.returncode == 0, f"Failed: {result.stderr}"

    output = json.loads(result.stdout)
    # Store A: 20, Store B: 60, total: 80
    assert output[0]['total_revenue'] == 80.0
    print("✓ Schema-level calculated fields work")


def test_parameter_type_checking():
    """Test that parameter types are enforced."""
    print("\nTesting parameter type checking...")

    csv_content = """timestamp,location,price,quantity
2024-01-01T10:00:00Z,Store A,10.0,2
"""

    dsl_content = """
    schema Sale {
        timestamp: timestamp
        location: string
        price: float
        quantity: float
    }

    pipeline TestPipeline using Sale {
        params {
            min_price: float = 0.0
        }
        filter(price >= param("min_price"))
        aggregate sum(price) as total_price
    }
    """

    # Provide wrong type (string where float expected)
    result = run_mtl(csv_content, dsl_content, params={'min_price': 'not_a_number'})
    assert result.returncode == 2, f"Should have failed with type error: {result.stderr}"
    assert "type_error" in result.stderr
    print("✓ Parameter type checking works")


def test_required_parameter_missing():
    """Test that missing required parameters cause error."""
    print("\nTesting required parameter validation...")

    csv_content = """timestamp,location,price,quantity
2024-01-01T10:00:00Z,Store A,10.0,2
"""

    dsl_content = """
    schema Sale {
        timestamp: timestamp
        location: string
        price: float
        quantity: float
    }

    pipeline TestPipeline using Sale {
        params {
            min_price: float  # No default, required
        }
        filter(price >= param("min_price"))
        aggregate sum(price) as total_price
    }
    """

    # Don't provide required parameter
    result = run_mtl(csv_content, dsl_content, params={})
    assert result.returncode == 1, f"Should have failed: {result.stderr}"
    assert "bad_dsl" in result.stderr
    print("✓ Required parameter validation works")


def test_multiple_calcs_with_lag():
    """Test multiple post-agg calcs with lag."""
    print("\nTesting multiple calcs with lag...")

    csv_content = """timestamp,location,price,quantity
2024-01-01T10:00:00Z,Store A,10.0,1
2024-01-01T11:00:00Z,Store A,20.0,1
2024-01-01T12:00:00Z,Store A,30.0,1
"""

    dsl_content = """
    schema Sale {
        timestamp: timestamp
        location: string
        price: float
        quantity: float
    }

    pipeline TestPipeline using Sale {
        aggregate sum(price) as total
        calc prev_total = lag(total, 1) : float @stage(post_agg)
        calc diff = total - coalesce(prev_total, 0.0) : float @stage(post_agg)
    }
    """

    result = run_mtl(csv_content, dsl_content)
    assert result.returncode == 0, f"Failed: {result.stderr}"

    output = json.loads(result.stdout)
    assert len(output) == 1  # No grouping, single result
    assert output[0]['total'] == 60.0  # 10+20+30
    assert output[0]['prev_total'] is None
    assert output[0]['diff'] == 60.0
    print("✓ Multiple calcs with lag work")


def test_cast_function():
    """Test explicit cast function."""
    print("\nTesting cast function...")

    csv_content = """timestamp,location,price,quantity
2024-01-01T10:00:00Z,Store A,10.9,2
"""

    dsl_content = """
    schema Sale {
        timestamp: timestamp
        location: string
        price: float
        quantity: float
    }

    pipeline TestPipeline using Sale {
        calc price_int = cast(price as int) : int @stage(pre)
        aggregate sum(price_int) as total_price_int
    }
    """

    result = run_mtl(csv_content, dsl_content)
    assert result.returncode == 0, f"Failed: {result.stderr}"

    output = json.loads(result.stdout)
    assert output[0]['total_price_int'] == 10  # 10.9 cast to int = 10
    print("✓ Cast function works")


def run_all_tests():
    """Run all tests."""
    tests = [
        test_basic_part1_compatibility,
        test_schema_with_inheritance,
        test_type_override_widening,
        test_illegal_type_override,
        test_pipeline_parameters,
        test_pre_stage_calculated_fields,
        test_post_agg_calculated_fields,
        test_lag_function,
        test_coalesce_function,
        test_schema_calculated_field,
        test_parameter_type_checking,
        test_required_parameter_missing,
        test_multiple_calcs_with_lag,
        test_cast_function,
    ]

    passed = 0
    failed = 0

    for test in tests:
        try:
            test()
            passed += 1
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"✗ {test.__name__} failed: {e}")
            import traceback
            traceback.print_exc()
            failed += 1

    print(f"\n{'='*50}")
    print(f"Results: {passed} passed, {failed} failed")
    print(f"{'='*50}")

    return failed == 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    success = run_all_tests()
    sys.exit(0 if success else 1)
